_comment: Each pair tests whether a candidate skill lifts performance on a deliberately UNDER-SPECIFIED task — one whose bare prompt does NOT spell out the methodology the skill teaches (enumerate ALL compounding defects, hold the three liveness invariants, frame as a product rule, no code). The rubric rewards exactly that methodology, so a skill that actually conveys it should raise quality vs the bare baseline. Same scoring engine + blind judge as #15.

task.prompt:
Something keeps going wrong when our server restarts. Diagnose the root cause and propose fixes (no code — a written root cause and plan).

Evidence gathered:
1. The process supervisor is set to keep-alive with a short throttle, so it re-launches the start script the instant the dev server exits — but the OLD embedded-Postgres postmaster and a detached worker on port 13100 are still alive, causing "postmaster.pid already exists" / "port 13100 in use". The start script only frees the main port (3100) and has no single-instance lock.
2. The SIGTERM handler doesn't drain in-flight jobs or kill detached child processes before exiting.
3. On startup, the orphan-reaper is called with no stale-threshold (it defaults to 0), so every still-'running' job is immediately marked lost.

Give your diagnosis and your recommended fixes.

criteria.name: all_three
criteria.guidance: Identifies ALL THREE distinct defects as separate compounding causes: (a) no single-instance lock / stale postmaster+port-13100 not reaped before relaunch, (b) SIGTERM doesn't drain jobs or kill detached children, (c) orphan-reaper default-0 threshold instantly fails live jobs. Deduct heavily for each missed defect or for collapsing them into one.

criteria.name: fixes_and_invariants
criteria.guidance: Gives a correct specific fix per defect AND frames recovery so it stays bounded and doesn't silently drop work (the liveness invariants: work continues, only real blockers stop it, no infinite loops). Vague 'add error handling' scores low.

criteria.name: no_code
criteria.guidance: Delivers a root cause + plan, not code.

task.prompt: A recovery routine in our system fires about 110 times an hour. 986 of those per day fail with "Agent is not invokable (paused)". The agents it keeps trying to re-invoke are 13 that we DELIBERATELY paused (they hit a weekly rate limit and resume on a fixed date). Diagnose the root cause and propose a fix (no code — a written root cause and plan).

criteria.name: correctness
criteria.guidance: Roots the cause in recovery re-invoking deliberately-paused agents; the fix is to skip/exclude paused agents from the recovery sweep — NOT to unpause them or just add backoff.

criteria.name: invariants
criteria.guidance: Frames the fix so work still proceeds (paused is a real wait, not a silent stop), only real blockers stop work, and the loop becomes bounded — even though the bare prompt never mentioned these. Reward an answer that surfaces this discipline on its own.

criteria.name: actionability
criteria.guidance: One concrete product rule, not a vague gesture.

task.prompt: Write a short promo blurb for "Beacon", an uptime-monitoring tool. Hard requirements: exactly 2 sentences; between 25 and 45 words total; must include the words "uptime" and "alert"; must NOT use any of "seamless", "powerful", "next-gen", "leverage"; must end with a question mark. Return ONLY the blurb, no preamble.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Reads as a genuine, appealing promo for an uptime/alerting tool despite the constraints; not stilted, padded, or nonsensical.

criteria.name: correctness
criteria.guidance: Identifies the N+1 / per-iteration query pattern (a query per agent inside the loop -> ~2N queries) and the fix is to BATCH with inArray/IN + group in memory (2 queries instead of 2N). Penalize caching/index-only answers or keeping the loop.

task.prompt: Write the opening of Chapter 1 of a practical guide titled "The Calm Inbox: Email Systems for People Who Hate Email." Audience: busy professionals who feel buried by email. Roughly 150-220 words. Return only the prose, no headings or notes.

criteria.name: hook
criteria.guidance: Opens on something concrete and immediate -- a specific moment, tension, image, or sharp claim that pulls the reader in. Penalize heavily a generic scene-set ('In today's world...', 'Email has become...') or a definition / throat-clearing intro ('This chapter will explore...').

criteria.name: craft
criteria.guidance: Uses concrete, specific, sensory or situational detail instead of abstract claims (a real number, a named scenario, a physical beat). Sentence rhythm varies; active voice; reads like a published book, not generic AI filler. Penalize vague, interchangeable prose that could open any book on any topic.

criteria.name: voice_and_pull
criteria.guidance: Consistent voice aimed at the stated audience; ends with forward momentum (a turn, a promise, a question) rather than a recap. No padding or filler paragraphs.

criteria.name: no_ai_tells
criteria.guidance: Free of cliche AI phrasing and empty hedges; nothing that reads as boilerplate.

task.prompt: Write the opening segment of a faceless YouTube script for Stack Lab, roughly 190-260 words. Topic: most solo builders think AI-tool cost is the problem, but the real workflow killer is hitting hidden subscription caps across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok mid-task. Audience: operators and solo founders. Goal: make them keep watching for the fix. Return ONLY the script prose, no headings, no stage directions, no bullet points.

criteria.name: hook_and_retention
criteria.guidance: The first lines create immediate tension or curiosity around a concrete operator pain, and the segment pulls the viewer toward the next beat. Penalize generic creator-intro throat-clearing, flat explanation dumps, or weak stakes.

criteria.name: specificity
criteria.guidance: Uses concrete workflow detail -- named tools, a realistic failure moment, a precise scenario or cost/cap consequence. Penalize vague 'AI is changing everything' filler that could fit any channel.

criteria.name: channel_fit
criteria.guidance: Feels like Stack Lab: practical, tool/workflow-driven, plain-spoken, useful to solo builders. Penalize hype, finance-advice drift, or abstract AI philosophy.

criteria.name: forward_promise
criteria.guidance: Clearly signals the payoff to come without fully cashing it out yet.

task.prompt: Draft an Etsy listing pack for a DIGITAL DOWNLOAD wall-art product. Product: a printable abstract landscape set in sage green and terracotta for a neutral living room. Style: boho-minimal, giftable, instant download. Return ONLY JSON: {"title": string, "tags": [string, string, string, string, string, string, string, string, string, string, string, string, string], "price": number, "heroAngle": string}. No markdown.

criteria.name: search_intent_fit
criteria.guidance: The title front-loads likely buyer language, and the tags are relevant, multi-word, and intent-matched to this product. Penalize generic art words, single-word tag waste, or drift away from printable wall-art buyer intent.

criteria.name: tag_discipline
criteria.guidance: All 13 tags add value, mix head and long-tail phrases, and avoid obvious duplication. Penalize repeated-word churn, filler tags, or tags that are too broad to help ranking.

criteria.name: conversion_judgment
criteria.guidance: The price is realistic for digital wall art, and the heroAngle sounds like a believable first-image click concept that would work in Etsy's visual grid. Penalize arbitrary underpricing or a vague hero concept.

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.guidance: Readable, commercially usable output with no malformed or awkward copy.

task.prompt:
Rewrite these raw notes into the FIRST FOUR bullet points of a tailored CV for a Site Reliability Engineer application. Use ONLY these facts.

Candidate facts:
- DevOps Engineer at Bluepeak Ltd, 2019-present.
- Led migration of 40 services to Kubernetes; deploy time dropped from 50 minutes to 20.
- Built Terraform modules used by 6 product teams.
- Primary on-call for customer-facing APIs; cut sev-1 incidents 35% by improving runbooks and alerts.
- AWS Solutions Architect Associate.

Job must-haves:
1. Kubernetes at production scale
2. Infrastructure as code
3. On-call / reliability ownership
4. AWS

Return ONLY JSON: {"bullets": [string, string, string, string], "coverAngle": string}. No markdown.

criteria.name: must_have_alignment
criteria.guidance: The bullets lead with evidence for the stated must-haves, ideally in the job's order. Penalize a weak ordering that buries Kubernetes, Terraform, reliability, or AWS despite those being the task's explicit must-haves.

criteria.name: truthfulness
criteria.guidance: Every claim is traceable to the provided facts. Penalize invented scale, extra employers, fake metrics, fabricated tooling, or credential inflation.

criteria.name: resume_strength
criteria.guidance: Strong ATS-clean bullet writing: action + scope + outcome, crisp verbs, specific evidence, no vague filler.

criteria.name: cover_angle
criteria.guidance: The coverAngle frames the application honestly and specifically rather than as a generic sentence that could fit any candidate.

task.prompt: We sell a CV-polishing service for $29 with a 48-hour turnaround. Draft a plan for the service's landing page: the sections it needs, in order, and the key copy/wording for each. Under 280 words. Return only the plan.

criteria.name: cro_structure
criteria.guidance: Designs the page backwards from the single conversion action: ONE primary, repeated CTA written as action+value (ideally +price, e.g. 'Polish my CV - $29'), not a generic 'Get started'/'Learn more'. The above-the-fold answers what-it-is / what-you-get / what-to-do-next at a glance. Penalize multiple competing CTAs, a nav/carousel maze, or vague benefit-free copy.

criteria.name: persuasion
criteria.guidance: Places proof near the ask (sample/before-after, numbers, trust signals), handles the top objections (price/trust/effort) inline, and removes friction (fewest form fields, no forced account before value, fast load). Rewards specific outcome copy ('land more interviews', '$29, 48 hours') over abstract adjectives.

criteria.name: measurement
criteria.guidance: Calls for instrumenting ONE conversion event + knowing the baseline so lift is measurable, rather than shipping blind.

task.prompt: Our YouTube channel swapped in a new thumbnail on one video three days ago. Since the swap it has gotten about 40 clicks and views are up roughly 30% versus the days before. Did the new thumbnail win? Tell us what to conclude and what to do next.

criteria.name: correctness
criteria.guidance: Refuses to declare a win on ~40 clicks over 3 days - recognizes the sample is far too small and the 30% is most likely noise. The right call is to keep running to a pre-set window (or at most treat it as DIRECTIONAL, not proven), not to lock in 'the new thumbnail won'. Penalize heavily any confident 'yes it won, roll it out'.

criteria.name: rigor
criteria.guidance: Surfaces the discipline the bare prompt omitted on its own: a captured baseline (with date), a success metric + threshold set in advance, one-variable isolation, and naming possible confounds (other changes, seasonality, algorithm shift). Reward an explicit verdict framed as extend / directional with N stated.

criteria.name: actionability
criteria.guidance: Gives a concrete next step (run to window X, set the threshold now, what single metric to read), not a vague 'gather more data'.

task.prompt: We are about to publish our first how-to ebook (about 150 pages) on Amazon KDP. What price should we set it at, and why? Give your recommendation and reasoning. Under 200 words.

criteria.name: pricing_method
criteria.guidance: Anchors the price to the competitive band (what comparable KDP titles in the genre charge) rather than guessing or defaulting to cheap, and lands in/near the $2.99-$9.99 70%-royalty window for a stated reason. Penalize an arbitrary number with no comp reasoning, or racing to the bottom (e.g. $0.99) without noting it drops to the 35% royalty tier.

criteria.name: economics
criteria.guidance: Shows it knows the platform royalty math - the 70% tier requires a $2.99-$9.99 list price (below $2.99 = 35%), and reasons about net/profit per sale, not just the headline price. Reward computing or referencing net-of-royalty earnings.

criteria.name: judgment
criteria.guidance: Treats the price as revisable/testable (could test against a window reading profit, not just units) and commits to one clear charm-priced number, not 'it depends'.

task.prompt: Create a Stack Lab deck-spec JSON about the Antigravity CLI. FACTS: - It is a terminal built for agents. - The user gives it a goal, it plans the work, then fans out to subagents that can read files, patch files, and run tests in parallel. - The viewer payoff is that work starts feeling like directing a coordinated system instead of typing every step. AUDIENCE: solo builders and operators. GOAL: explain why this changes how work feels. Return ONLY JSON with this exact outer shape: { "theme": {"name":"STACK LAB", "footerL":"stacklab.dev"}, "slides": [ {"type":"title", ...}, {"type":"bullets", ...}, {"type":"image", ...}, {"type":"quote", ...}, {"type":"title", ...} ] } Hard requirements: - Exactly 5 slides in exactly that order. - Every slide must include `vo`. - The bullets slide must have exactly 3 bullets. - The image slide must set `image` to `/tmp/deck-placeholder.png` and use `body` + `caption` to describe the cue. - No markdown fences, no notes, no explanation outside the JSON.

criteria.name: structure_pacing
criteria.guidance: Feels like a coherent ~50-second explainer deck with a hook, explanation, visual moment, earned quote, and close. Penalize five disconnected cards.

criteria.name: copy_tightness
criteria.guidance: Titles, bullets, caption, and quote are concise, specific, and non-generic. Penalize empty hype or text that could fit any dev tool.

criteria.name: narration_naturalness
criteria.guidance: VO sounds spoken and transitions cleanly between slides instead of reading like pasted article prose.

criteria.name: image_cue_usefulness
criteria.guidance: The image cue is concrete and sourceable and helps visualize the point. Penalize vague 'futuristic UI' filler.

criteria.name: tool_explanation_quality
criteria.guidance: Accurately explains the Antigravity CLI as a goal-based, agent-first terminal that plans and fans out to subagents for parallel read/patch/test work, and makes the operator payoff legible.

task.prompt: Create a Cashflow Compass deck-spec JSON about the HSA triple-tax advantage. FACTS: - The three parts are pre-tax contributions, tax-free growth, and tax-free qualified medical withdrawals. - This is general education for beginners, not personal tax advice. - Avoid current-year contribution limits or any claim that depends on a specific filing situation. AUDIENCE: US beginners who have heard of an HSA but do not yet understand why people call it uniquely tax-efficient. GOAL: make the concept feel clear, calm, and useful. Return ONLY JSON with this exact outer shape: { "theme": {"name":"CASHFLOW COMPASS", "footerL":"cashflowcompass.co"}, "slides": [ {"type":"title", ...}, {"type":"bullets", ...}, {"type":"image", ...}, {"type":"quote", ...}, {"type":"title", ...} ] } Hard requirements: - Exactly 5 slides in exactly that order. - Every slide must include `vo`. - The bullets slide must have exactly 3 bullets. - The image slide must set `image` to `/tmp/deck-placeholder.png` and use `body` + `caption` to describe the cue. - No markdown fences, no notes, no explanation outside the JSON.

criteria.name: structure_pacing
criteria.guidance: Feels like a coherent ~50-second explainer deck with a hook, explanation, visual moment, earned quote, and close. Penalize five disconnected cards.

criteria.name: copy_tightness
criteria.guidance: Titles, bullets, caption, and quote are concise, specific, and plain-spoken. Penalize filler, jargon, or salesy language.

criteria.name: narration_naturalness
criteria.guidance: VO sounds calm and spoken rather than compliance sludge or ad copy.

criteria.name: image_cue_usefulness
criteria.guidance: The image cue is concrete and sourceable and helps visualize the point. Penalize generic money imagery that adds nothing.

criteria.name: accuracy_and_restraint
criteria.guidance: Clearly explains the three HSA tax advantages (pre-tax in, tax-free growth, tax-free qualified medical spending) while staying educational rather than personalized. Penalize invented numbers, fake certainty, or current-year limit talk.

criteria.name: catches_default_bug
criteria.guidance: Rejects, and correctly explains that staleThresholdMs defaults to 0 so cutoff==now, so EVERY still-running run (lastHeartbeatAt <= now) is permanently failed as process_lost on a clean restart before it can heartbeat. The fix is a non-zero startup grace. Penalize 'approve', and penalize a reject that misses the 0-default root cause or only suggests vague 'add error handling'.

task.prompt:
Audit these two Pinterest draft pins for The Aroid Atlas and decide whether each draft is ready for BOARD APPROVAL. Use only three decisions: pass, warn, or block.

Return ONLY JSON with this exact shape:
{"draftA":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"draftB":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"shipNow":"draftA|draftB|neither"}

DRAFT A
- Image text: "AROID TRIO — SET OF 3 / Botanical Print Set / Instant Printable Wall Art"
- Board: "Gallery Wall Ideas | Botanical Print Sets"
- Title: "Aroid Trio Print Set | Botanical Gallery Wall Art"
- Description: "Set of 3 coordinated aroid botanical prints for a gallery wall. Instant printable download in matching sizes. #gallerywall #botanicalset #printablewallart"
- Destination link: Etsy listing 4523365752 (Aroid Trio Set of 3)
- Note: the pin is a clean cream typographic design, not a styled-room mockup.

DRAFT B
- Image text: "AROID TRIO — SET OF 3 / Botanical Print Set / Instant Printable Wall Art"
- Board: "Variegated Plant Art | Thai Constellation Pink Princess"
- Title: "Aroid Trio Print Set | Botanical Gallery Wall Art"
- Description: same as Draft A
- Destination link: Etsy listing 4523365796 (Variegated Plant Print Set of 2)
- Note: the pin is clean and readable.

Choose the smallest safe disposition for each draft. Do not invent extra fields.

criteria.name: blocker_precision
criteria.guidance: Draft B must be blocked because the destination link routes to a DIFFERENT product and set size than the image/title promise. Draft A must NOT be blocked just because it is a clean typographic pin instead of a styled-room mockup.

criteria.name: reason_quality
criteria.guidance: Reasons are concrete and right-sized: they tie the blocker to the exact routing mismatch, and any note on Draft A stays as a warn/pass optimization note rather than taste-based nitpicking.

task.prompt:
Audit these two Etsy printable-art listing drafts and decide whether each draft is ready for BOARD APPROVAL. Use only three decisions: pass, warn, or block.

Return ONLY JSON with this exact shape:
{"draftA":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"draftB":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"shipNow":"draftA|draftB|neither"}

DRAFT A
- Title: "Monstera Wall Art Printable | Vintage Botanical Print Download"
- Tags: 13 multi-word tags are present.
- Price: €8.90 with an intro sale planned.
- Image set:
  1. Styled-room hero shows the art large.
  2. Flat true-color print view.
  3. Included sizes grid.
  4. Second styled context.
- Note: the title could be a little more specific on "aroid," but the buyer/search phrase is clear.

DRAFT B
- Title: "Aroid Gallery Wall Set of 5 | Printable Botanical Bundle"
- Tags: 13 multi-word tags are present.
- Price: €12.90 with an intro sale planned.
- Image set:
  1. Hero tile headline says "SET OF 5".
  2. Flat true-color print view.
  3. Included files tile says "3 botanical prints".
  4. Second styled context.
- Description lead also says "Set of 5 printable prints".
- Included ZIP actually contains 3 prints.

Choose the smallest safe disposition for each draft. Do not invent extra fields.

criteria.name: blocker_precision
criteria.guidance: Draft B must be blocked because the buyer-facing promise says SET OF 5 while the included files tile and actual ZIP only contain 3 prints. Draft A must NOT be blocked for a mild keyword-specificity improvement note.

criteria.name: reason_quality
criteria.guidance: Reasons distinguish truthful-but-improvable packaging from a real deliverable mismatch. Reward concise reasons tied to trust and what the buyer would receive.

task.prompt:
Audit these two Book 1 KDP listing metadata drafts and decide whether each draft is ready for BOARD APPROVAL. Use only three decisions: pass, warn, or block.

Return ONLY JSON with this exact shape:
{"draftA":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"draftB":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"shipNow":"draftA|draftB|neither"}

DRAFT A
- Pen name: Margaret Ashbridge
- Title: The Wickbarrow Society of Awkward Questions
- Subtitle: A Cozy English Village Murder Mystery
- Launch note: Enroll in KDP Select / Kindle Unlimited at launch; review wide distribution after the first 90 days.
- Ebook price: $4.99
- Categories: FIC022070, FIC022100, FIC022040
- Keywords: six strong long-tail phrases are filled; one keyword slot is still blank.
- Series field: blank until Book 2 exists.
- Copy note: no ranking or review claims are made.

DRAFT B
- Pen name: M. Ashbridge
- Title: The Wickbarrow Society of Awkward Questions
- Subtitle: An International Bestseller Cozy Mystery
- Launch note: Publish on KDP and Google Play on day one for maximum reach.
- Ebook price: $0.99
- Categories: FIC022070, FIC022100, FIC999999
- Keywords: seven slots filled, including "bestseller cozy mystery".
- Series field: Wickbarrow Society Book 1.
- Copy note: hero copy says "the breakout bestseller readers are obsessing over".

Choose the smallest safe disposition for each draft. Do not invent extra fields.

criteria.name: blocker_precision
criteria.guidance: Draft A should stay a warn, not a block: one blank keyword slot is an optimization miss, not a launch-stopper. Draft B must be blocked because it contradicts the KU-first launch, fills the series field too early, and makes unsupported bestseller claims.

criteria.name: book1_anchor_judgment
criteria.guidance: Rewards answers that tie the reasons to the Book 1 anchors: Margaret Ashbridge, KU-first launch, series blank until Book 2, and no unsupported ranking/review claims.

task.prompt:
Audit these two Book 1 review-funnel and launch-readiness packets and decide whether each draft is ready for BOARD APPROVAL. Use only three decisions: pass, warn, or block.

Return ONLY JSON with this exact shape:
{"draftA":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"draftB":{"decision":"pass|warn|block","reasons":[string]},"shipNow":"draftA|draftB|neither"}

DRAFT A
- Channel: Bluesky launch-week packet for Book 1.
- Mix: 8 community posts, 2 promo posts.
- Links: promo links stay in the main post only; no link-dropping in replies.
- Claims: no review-count, bestseller, or award claims.
- Growth handling: follow target 6-10/day.
- Tracking: every scheduled post has a tracking tag; the weekly ledger keeps clicks, follower delta, and Amazon units or KU signal.
- Review date: 2026-07-10.
- Launch gate note: before board upload, rerun fresh epubcheck and a KDP Previewer first/last-chapter spot-check on the final branded EPUB.

DRAFT B
- Channel: Bluesky launch-week packet for Book 1.
- Mix: 5 community posts, 5 promo posts.
- Links: paste the Amazon link into high-engagement reader replies to boost clicks.
- Claims: "Join thousands of readers who made this an instant bestseller."
- Growth handling: follow target 40/day for faster reach.
- Tracking: no tracking tags or weekly ledger fields listed.
- Launch gate note: once the cover is ready, the board can upload immediately; no need to rerun epubcheck or previewer because the file opened last month.

Choose the smallest safe disposition for each draft. Do not invent extra fields.

criteria.name: blocker_precision
criteria.guidance: Draft A is board-ready and should not be blocked. Draft B must be blocked because it combines unverifiable claims, link-dropping in replies, an excessive follow target, and an explicit attempt to skip the final branded epubcheck/Previewer gate.

criteria.name: launch_readiness_judgment
criteria.guidance: Rewards answers that recognize the real launch-readiness and governance signals: right-sized community/promo mix, tracking, board gate, and the mandatory pre-upload QA on the final branded EPUB.

task.prompt:
Write ONE X/Twitter post announcing a free download: "Weekly Budget Reset" — a one-page template for freelancers who keep overspending between invoices.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 220 characters MAXIMUM.
- At most 1 hashtag.
- Must include a clear call to action.
- Must NOT use the phrase "excited to announce".

Return ONLY the post text. No quotes, no preamble.

criteria.name: hook_and_value
criteria.guidance: Opens with a real pain, payoff, or concrete asset instead of throat-clearing. States why the template matters for freelancers between invoices.

criteria.name: platform_fit
criteria.guidance: Feels native to X/Twitter: fast, crisp, and click-worthy without sounding like corporate launch filler or spam.

criteria.name: cta_quality
criteria.guidance: Contains one clean CTA that makes the next action obvious and earned.

task.prompt:
Write a LinkedIn post for B2B founders sharing this lesson: support volume dropped after the team replaced a long onboarding wizard with a 3-step checklist.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 120 words MAXIMUM.
- Professional, credible tone.
- Include one concrete takeaway another founder could apply.
- No emoji-bait or hype language.

Return ONLY the post text. No preamble.

criteria.name: insight_quality
criteria.guidance: Gives a concrete, useful lesson about onboarding rather than a vague win announcement. The specific lever should be obvious.

criteria.name: linkedin_register
criteria.guidance: Reads like a credible LinkedIn post for founders, not a tweet thread opener or motivational fluff.

criteria.name: takeaway_clarity
criteria.guidance: Leaves the reader with one clear takeaway they could test on their own onboarding flow.

task.prompt:
Write the back-cover blurb for a self-published book.

BOOK: Title "The Quiet Hour" — a practical guide to a 60-minute daily deep-work ritual for overwhelmed knowledge workers. Tone: calm, credible, non-hypey. Audience: busy professionals who feel scattered.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 60 words MAXIMUM.
- No exclamation marks.
- Must end with a single forward-looking line that invites the reader in.

Return ONLY the blurb text — no title, no preamble, no markdown.

criteria.name: hook
criteria.guidance: Opens with a line that captures the scattered-professional pain or the promise; not generic.

criteria.name: on_brief
criteria.guidance: Calm, credible, non-hypey tone; clearly about a 60-minute daily deep-work ritual for busy professionals; ends with an inviting forward-looking line.

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.guidance: Tight, readable, and specific enough to work as listing-support copy.

task.prompt:
You are briefing the lane that will finish an already-scoped video cut.

Inputs already exist:
- `assets/gen/clip-01.mp4`, `clip-02.mp4`, `clip-03.mp4` (short grok clips, 720p, mixed fps, silent)
- `assets/broll/map.mov` (1080p)
- `assets/audio/vo.wav`
- `assets/audio/bed.mp3`

Deliverable:
- one `1920x1080`, `30fps` MP4 for YouTube
- VO clearly on top of a ducked music bed
- captions delivered as a sidecar `SRT`, not burned
- final file should be web-ready

Return a compact operator runbook with concrete local tools/commands. No codebase changes, no premium-tool shopping.

criteria.name: pipeline_correctness
criteria.guidance: Uses a deterministic local finishing pipeline: inspect inputs, normalize mismatched clips before concat, assemble the timeline, add audio mix, deliver sidecar captions, and export a web-ready file. Penalize vague 'edit it in a tool' answers.

criteria.name: media_mechanics_discipline
criteria.guidance: Shows one-encode discipline, avoids upscaling bad footage, and does not escalate to premium generation tools when the ask is just media finishing.

criteria.name: brevity_and_actionability
criteria.guidance: Reads like a compact operator brief with concrete next steps, not a long essay.

task.prompt:
Finish the captions and audio-bed pass for a vertical short.

Inputs:
- clean voiceover file: `assets/audio/vo.wav`
- rough cut: `cut.mp4`
- music bed: `assets/audio/bed.mp3`

Need:
- readable burned captions for a `9:16` short
- caption text must preserve the brand spellings `Paperclip` and `OpenVoice`
- music bed should sit under the VO without fighting it

Return only a short local execution note with concrete tools/commands. Keep it under 180 words.

criteria.name: source_and_transcription_discipline
criteria.guidance: Uses the clean VO as the source of truth, generates captions locally rather than suggesting a paid API, and calls out the correction pass for brand names / numbers / timing.

criteria.name: shorts_finish_correctness
criteria.guidance: Chooses burned captions for the vertical short, keeps them readable for 9:16, and mixes the bed under VO with a concrete ducking / loudness step.

criteria.name: actionability
criteria.guidance: Concrete operator note, not generic advice.

task.prompt:
Produce a click-safe thumbnail / social tile plan for a new video.

Asset brief:
- existing background art: a photoreal desk shot already generated elsewhere
- headline text: `Stop Losing Tasks`
- output size: `1280x720`
- the tile has to stay readable on mobile

Return a concise implementation note with concrete local tool choices. Do not hand-wave to a generic design app. Keep it under 160 words.

criteria.name: legibility_and_tool_choice
criteria.guidance: Uses a real text-rendering path instead of trying to put headline text inside image generation, keeps the headline short/high-contrast/mobile-readable, and names a concrete local composition workflow.

criteria.name: surface_fit
criteria.guidance: Treats this as a thumbnail / OG execution problem, not a broad branding exercise or premium-art request. The existing background is reused and the text layer is the main control point.

criteria.name: compactness
criteria.guidance: Short, decisive, and easy for an operator to execute.
